questions.question: What is a Bug life cycle?
questions.answer:
1. New

  • A tester finds a defect and logs it in a bug tracking tool.
  • Status: New

2. Assigned

  • The bug is assigned to a developer or team for investigation.
  • Status: Assigned

3. Open

  • The developer starts analyzing and working on the bug.
  • Status: Open

4. Fixed

  • Developer resolves the issue and marks it as fixed.
  • Status: Fixed

5. Retest

  • Tester verifies the fix in a new build.
  • Status: Retest

6. Verified

  • Tester confirms the bug is actually fixed.
  • Status: Verified

7. Closed

  • The bug is officially closed after successful verification.
  • Status: Closed

Possible Alternate States (Important)

These depend on real-world scenarios:

  • Rejected → Not a bug (works as expected)
  • Duplicate → Same as an already reported bug
  • Deferred → Will be fixed later (low priority)
  • Reopened → Bug still exists after fix
  • Cannot Reproduce → Not reproducible by developer/tester
  • Blocked → Cannot proceed due to dependency
